terms.definition: Automated test exercising one small unit (function/class) in isolation, with external dependencies mocked, running in milliseconds and deterministic.
Heuristic that says a healthy test portfolio is mostly unit tests (fast, many), some integration tests, and a small number of end-to-end tests.
Arrange / Act / Assert — the standard three-phase structure of a unit test.
BDD vocabulary for the same three phases as AAA; popularised by Cucumber/Gherkin.
The de facto Python testing framework; auto-discovers `test_*.py` files, supports plain `assert`, fixtures, parametrize, markers, and a large plugin ecosystem.
One test body executed multiple times with different input/expected pairs via `@pytest.mark.parametrize`.
A test added to reproduce a fixed bug so it can never come back unnoticed; usually labelled with the bug ID.
A test whose outcome varies across runs without a code change; usually caused by shared state, real time, networks or unordered iteration.
How pytest finds tests automatically (by default: files named `test_*.py`/`*_test.py`, functions named `test_*`, classes named `Test*`).
